title: Inventory Item Tracker with Enum and Struct
prompt_md:
## Goal
Combine a scoped enum with a struct to model inventory items. Read items, print a report, and total their quantities.

Rarity codes: `0` = Common, `1` = Rare, `2` = Legendary.

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 5), the number of items
- Next `N` groups of two lines:
  - item name (single word)
  - two integers: `quantity` (1-999) and `rarity_code` (0, 1, or 2)

## Output
- `N` lines, one per item:
  ```
  <name> x<quantity> [<Rarity>]
  ```
  where `<Rarity>` is `Common`, `Rare`, or `Legendary`.
- Then a blank line, then:
  ```
  Total items: <sum_of_quantities>
  ```

## Constraints
- Define an `enum class Rarity` and a `struct Item` holding the name, quantity, and rarity.
- Map a rarity to its display string with a function that switches on the enum. Convert each code with `static_cast<Rarity>`.
- Do not use arrays or `std::vector`; process each item as you read it.

test_cases.label: Sample -- three items of different rarity
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Sword x5 [Common]
Shield x2 [Rare]
Crown x1 [Legendary]

Total items: 8

test_cases.label: Hidden -- five items all legendary
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Helm x3 [Legendary]
Bow x7 [Legendary]
Ring x1 [Legendary]
Amulet x4 [Legendary]
Boots x2 [Legendary]

Total items: 17

title: Generic Pair Statistics with Class Template
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write your own class template `Pair` holding two values of the same type, plus a function template that returns the larger of the two, then use it on integer and double pairs.

## Input
- Line 1: two integers (pair 1)
- Line 2: two integers (pair 2)
- Line 3: two doubles (pair 3)

## Output
Three lines:
```
Int pair 1 max: <value>
Int pair 2 max: <value>
Double pair max: <value>
```
Doubles use default `std::cout` formatting.

## Constraints
- Define a class template `Pair<T>` with members `first` and `second`.
- Write a function template returning the larger member (return `first` if they are equal).
- Use CTAD for at least one of the three pairs (omit its template argument and let the compiler deduce it).
- Do not use `std::max`, `std::pair`, arrays, or `std::vector`.

test_cases.label: Sample -- mixed values
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Int pair 1 max: 20
Int pair 2 max: 5
Double pair max: 2.7

test_cases.label: Hidden -- equal values and negatives
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Int pair 1 max: 7
Int pair 2 max: -3
Double pair max: -1.5

test_cases.label: Hidden -- large values and second larger
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Int pair 1 max: 1000
Int pair 2 max: 100
Double pair max: 0.999
